title: Pulpit Friction

jokes.text: Really? You want to throw something out just because it's a little old?
jokes.setup: Marge suggests getting a new couch because the old one is beaten up
jokes.punchline: Homer, who is old and beaten up himself, objects to throwing out old things
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer self-awareness comedy where his objection perfectly applies to himself. Good character integration and ironic structure.

jokes.text: Are there any two words more exciting than 'couch shopping'?
jokes.setup: Normal family preparing to furniture shop
jokes.punchline: Marge's earnest excitement about the mundane activity of couch shopping
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Marge's domestic enthusiasm. Well-integrated with her personality.

jokes.text: And, you know, Marge, those salespeople weren't really our friends.
jokes.setup: Marge wanting to visit salespeople again
jokes.punchline: Homer's matter-of-fact deflation of Marge's naive social assumption
jokes.explanation: Nice dynamic between Homer's cynicism and Marge's optimism about human nature.

jokes.text: They charged a deposit. / They had to. It came from corporate. / That's right, sweetie, it always comes from corporate.
jokes.setup: Marge defending the fabric sample deposit
jokes.punchline: Homer's weary recognition of corporate blame-shifting
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation about modern customer service excuses, with good family dynamic and escalating recognition.

jokes.text: Chumps. Don't everyone know that all New York furniture is full of bedbugs?
jokes.setup: Delivery truck heading to Springfield
jokes.punchline: Delivery worker's casual revelation about bedbug infestation
jokes.explanation: Good plot setup that becomes funny in retrospect. Works as both exposition and character moment.

jokes.text: It's forgot abouten!
jokes.setup: Worker dismissing the bedbug problem
jokes.punchline: Malapropism combining 'forgotten' and 'about it'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that sounds like authentic working-class speech pattern. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: Gentlemen, the fort is complete and will stand forever. No way the Nazis will get in here. / Oh, I thought we were the Nazis.
jokes.setup: Boys building a couch cushion fort, Bart declares it Nazi-proof
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's confused assumption about which side they're playing
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy subverting children's innocent war games. Perfect character moment for Milhouse's confusion.

jokes.text: Ew, just hearing about them makes me itch. / Ooh, ooh. Funny how the brain does that, huh? / It's making me see them. / The brain is so stupid.
jokes.setup: News report about bedbugs
jokes.punchline: Homer's pseudo-intellectual analysis followed by immediate contradiction
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer pseudo-intellectualism immediately undercut by his own experience. Good escalating structure.

jokes.text: Those are bedbugs!
jokes.setup: Homer claiming his brain is making him see things
jokes.punchline: Lisa's matter-of-fact confirmation that they're real
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing and delivery to puncture Homer's rationalization. Lisa as the voice of reason.

jokes.text: Probably one of Bart's dirty friends. / Boy, why are your friends so dirty? / Don't know. Why are your friends such drunks? / Touché.
jokes.setup: Parents blaming Bart's friends for the infestation
jokes.punchline: Bart's devastating counter-attack about Homer's drinking buddies
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reversal with Bart turning parental criticism back on them. Homer's 'Touché' shows even he recognizes the perfect comeback.

jokes.text: Bring out your bed!
jokes.setup: Springfield dealing with bedbug crisis
jokes.punchline: Reference to 'bring out your dead' from Monty Python
jokes.explanation: Solid reference humor that enhances the plague atmosphere. Works for those who get the reference.

jokes.text: You see, behold the power of modified bear pheromones.
jokes.setup: Frink claiming to have bedbug solution
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous pseudo-scientific solution involving bear pheromones
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frink moment with characteristic pseudo-science. The bear pheromones setup pays off later.

jokes.text: 'Tweren't us. We don't even have beds. We sleep in washtubs and coffins.
jokes.setup: Someone suggesting the poorest family caused the bedbug problem
jokes.punchline: Cletus's matter-of-fact description of their poverty
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment that's both funny and somewhat dark. The escalation from washtubs to coffins is perfect.

jokes.text: Why won't somebody blame the children?
jokes.setup: Community trying to find someone to blame for bedbugs
jokes.punchline: Helen's predictable attempt to blame children for everything
jokes.explanation: Perfect character consistency. Helen always blames children, so her frustration when they're not being blamed is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: You know, in the Middle Ages, people took refuge in church to escape the bubonic plague, but that made the plague spread even faster.
jokes.setup: People gathering in church to escape bedbugs
jokes.punchline: Lisa's historical knowledge showing this will make things worse
jokes.explanation: Smart historical parallel that's both educational and ominous. Perfect Lisa character moment showing her intelligence.

jokes.text: The Epistle of Jeremy is often cited as the most difficult book of the Apocrypha. But to me, none of the anagignoskomena are more contemplative than the book of Tobit.
jokes.setup: Crisis situation where people need comfort
jokes.punchline: Lovejoy's completely tone-deaf theological lecture using obscure terms
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lovejoy's disconnect from his congregation's needs. Sets up the contrast with the new minister.

jokes.text: Mind if I p-p-pray through?
jokes.setup: New minister arriving during crisis
jokes.punchline: Play on 'play through' golf term adapted for religious context
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that immediately establishes the new minister's casual, modern approach.

jokes.text: God's soldier in cardigan.
jokes.setup: New minister's casual appearance
jokes.punchline: Mock-heroic description of his cardigan sweater
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy in contrasting military imagery with cozy clothing. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Now, for chili, that's just dandy, but I brought you a little backup.
jokes.setup: Describing the situation as 'five-alarm'
jokes.punchline: Moe's literal interpretation relating to chili peppers
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Moe's literal-minded approach to metaphors.

jokes.text: He was the number two man from Shelbyville. Introduced angle parking. We got in six more cars.
jokes.setup: Introducing the impressive new minister
jokes.punchline: His great achievement was a minor parking innovation
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of expectations. The mundane nature of the achievement contrasts with the build-up.

jokes.text: The posture, the haircut, the tiny feet. It's amazing they hold you up. But they do. They do.
jokes.setup: Hooper complimenting Lovejoy
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment about his tiny feet, then doubling down
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment showing Hooper's passive-aggressive nature disguised as friendliness.

jokes.text: It's like he's selling silver polish at the state fair.
jokes.setup: Hooper's overly charming presentation style
jokes.punchline: Marge's specific comparison to a particular type of salesman
jokes.explanation: Excellent specific comparison that perfectly captures a particular type of over-enthusiastic salesperson.

jokes.text: Church should make you happy, like a warm mug of soup. Why does it taste better from a mug? I don't know, but it does.
jokes.setup: Explaining his philosophy of church
jokes.punchline: Getting sidetracked by the soup-in-mug observation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Hooper's scattered, relatable thought process.

jokes.text: God help me, I'm paying attention.
jokes.setup: Hooper's engaging preaching style
jokes.punchline: Homer's shocked realization that he's actually listening
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer line showing his usual church disengagement. The 'God help me' adds ironic layer.

jokes.text: Hilarious film; showed a whole new side of De Niro. At first, he couldn't stand his son-in-law, but then they patch things up in a comical fashion, remember?
jokes.setup: Using pop culture to explain religious concepts
jokes.punchline: Oversimplified plot summary of Meet the Parents
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment showing modern minister approach, but the execution is a bit verbose.

jokes.text: Go home and watch one of the Die Hard movies on TV. They're always on, and they're always good.
jokes.setup: Modern minister dismissing congregation early
jokes.punchline: Specific observation about Die Hard movies' constant TV presence
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor that rings true. Many viewers will relate to this TV phenomenon.

jokes.text: And, ladies, drop in on a neighbor's open house just to see what they did with their sun room.
jokes.setup: Continuing to suggest Sunday activities
jokes.punchline: Very specific suburban lady activity
jokes.explanation: Good observational detail about suburban social behavior.

jokes.text: We're here every Sunday, just like Snoopy in color.
jokes.setup: Assuring congregation they'll be there next week
jokes.punchline: Comparison to newspaper comic strip reliability
jokes.explanation: Clever comparison showing reliability through reference to Sunday comics tradition.

jokes.text: My wedding ring is creating a glare. Can you hold it?
jokes.setup: Helen watching the charismatic new minister
jokes.punchline: Transparent excuse to remove her wedding ring
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Helen's immediate attraction and poor excuse-making.

jokes.text: This is the worst 17th Sunday in Ordinary Time ever.
jokes.setup: Lovejoy's congregation abandoning him for the new minister
jokes.punchline: Overly specific liturgical calendar reference for his complaint
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment combining Lovejoy's theological precision with his petty complaints.

jokes.text: They give you a bag of the bedbugs they killed.
jokes.setup: Family returned from bedbug treatment
jokes.punchline: Absurd customer service gesture of providing the dead bugs
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor about inappropriate customer service. Has the ring of bizarre truth.

jokes.text: Homer's oatmeal, meet your raisins.
jokes.setup: Homer looking at bag of dead bedbugs
jokes.punchline: Treating the bugs as food ingredients
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer gross-out moment, though relies on 'Homer will eat anything' trope.

jokes.text: I don't want a comedian knowing my dress size.
jokes.setup: Marge needing to retrieve her wedding dress from Krusty
jokes.punchline: Specific feminine anxiety about male comedian judging her
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific anxiety that feels authentic to Marge's personality and gender.

jokes.text: Wait a minute! This has oats in it! / Ooh, a nose hair trimmer.
jokes.setup: Homer discovering items in his dry cleaning
jokes.punchline: More excited about nose hair trimmer than concerned about food contamination
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's skewed priorities and fascination with gadgets.

jokes.text: Where's my mail-order bride?
jokes.setup: Krusty using wedding dress in sketch
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to mail-order bride for his act
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Krusty's morally questionable comedy material.

jokes.text: I threw it out the window during my post-show hissy fit.
jokes.setup: Asking what happened to the dress
jokes.punchline: Krusty's casual admission of tantrum behavior
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Krusty's unprofessional behavior and emotional instability.

jokes.text: Wooden. Plastic. / Wooden. Plastic. / Why do we always argue over coffee stirrers?
jokes.setup: Two overly proper men in discussion
jokes.punchline: Absurdly petty argument topic revealed
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing how these two uptight characters would argue about trivial details.

jokes.text: That is the wrong thing to say to a snake handler.
jokes.setup: Reverend Hooper describing his laid-back Protestant faith
jokes.punchline: Moe's warning about his extreme religious practices
jokes.explanation: Great setup/payoff revealing Moe practices extreme fundamentalism. Good tension and character revelation.

jokes.text: Ah, this thing's just loaded with rock salt. I use it to keep the raccoons out of my fridge. And, of course, to coat the rims of my margarita glasses.
jokes.setup: Moe pulling a gun on the minister
jokes.punchline: Casual explanation of the gun's mundane uses
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from threatening to practical to festive. Very Moe-like resourcefulness.

jokes.text: And if it's such a 'good book,' how come there's no blurbs on the back? Not even David Sedaris. And he'll flack anything.
jokes.setup: Homer critiquing the Bible
jokes.punchline: Applying modern book marketing standards to scripture
jokes.explanation: Brilliant modern critique of religious marketing. The David Sedaris reference is perfectly specific.

jokes.text: Seriously, 'pews'?
jokes.setup: Homer continuing church criticism
jokes.punchline: Objecting to the name of church seating
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay questioning religious terminology. Excellent economy - just one word plus delivery.

jokes.text: No, not at all. It's like a sexton or a rector.
jokes.setup: Homer asking if deacon is like weird Catholic priest things
jokes.punchline: Hooper's response with equally suggestive-sounding church positions
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay about religious titles that sound suggestive to modern ears.

jokes.text: But, as we say in the snow plow business, I'm your astronaut.
jokes.setup: Homer agreeing to be deacon
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical snow plow business saying
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist humor with callback to Homer's brief snow plow career. Perfectly nonsensical.

jokes.text: Just looking for the bathroom. / This is not the bathroom! / Aw, but this paper towel feels so good.
jokes.setup: Homer walking in on Marge trying on dress
jokes.punchline: Homer's distraction by the fabric texture
jokes.explanation: Good Homer moment showing his tactile focus overriding social awareness.

jokes.text: Well, then, you shouldn't feel bad, because I can't imagine myself ever getting married.
jokes.setup: Marge wanting Lisa to wear the wedding dress someday
jokes.punchline: Lisa's matter-of-fact rejection of marriage as concept
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lisa's progressive, independent thinking.

jokes.text: That would save us several hundred bucks. / Tell her she's wrong!
jokes.setup: Lisa saying she won't get married
jokes.punchline: Homer's practical excitement vs Marge's traditional concern
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Homer and Marge's reactions, showing their different priorities.

jokes.text: We let you get up early to watch the Royal Wedding. / Hey, I have a weakness for pomp.
jokes.setup: Homer arguing Lisa should want to get married
jokes.punchline: Lisa's intellectual distinction between ceremony and institution
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa response showing her intellectual sophistication and honest self-awareness.

jokes.text: So, you see, what Jesus is saying really can be explained by an episode of Californication.
jokes.setup: Modern minister explaining religious concepts
jokes.punchline: Using inappropriate HBO show to explain Jesus
jokes.explanation: Good satire of modern ministers trying too hard to be relevant with inappropriate pop culture references.

jokes.text: You bring in the lost sheep, and the others will follow. / Lost sheep. Yes! Sis boom bah!
jokes.setup: Moe explaining strategy for making Homer deacon
jokes.punchline: Homer treating religious metaphor like cheerleading
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's complete misunderstanding of religious terminology.

jokes.text: Heading for the door, pushing the handle. Left foot across the threshold. Now the right. Walking to car. Stepped in hole. Twisted ankle. Tremendous pain. Keep it elevated.
jokes.setup: Lovejoy leaving church in defeat
jokes.punchline: Overly detailed narration of his exit, then immediate comeuppance
jokes.explanation: Excellent comedy escalation with the detailed narration building to physical comedy payoff.

jokes.text: Church bulletin? Church bulletin? Find out in whose loving memory this week's service is. The answer may shock you.
jokes.setup: Lovejoy reduced to handing out bulletins
jokes.punchline: Desperate tabloid-style marketing for church bulletin
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of clickbait marketing applied to religious materials. Shows Lovejoy's desperation.

jokes.text: And now, finally, I believe in something bigger than myself. / The only thing bigger than you is you tomorrow. / Why, you little... lamb.
jokes.setup: Homer expressing newfound spirituality
jokes.punchline: Bart's cutting insult, Homer catching himself mid-strangling
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup/payoff with perfect character dynamics. Homer's religious restraint subverting the classic strangling.

jokes.text: Only God can do that.
jokes.setup: Homer stopping himself from strangling Bart
jokes.punchline: Twisted religious logic about who can kill his son
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy combining Homer's religious awakening with his murderous impulses toward Bart.

jokes.text: Someone finally took one. / I'm mentioned in it 'cause I'm sick.
jokes.setup: Lovejoy excited someone took a bulletin
jokes.punchline: Man only interested because he's mentioned for being sick
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Lovejoy's hopes. The man's self-interest is perfectly mundane.

jokes.text: Using something this town has never, ever seen-- good, honest police work.
jokes.setup: Wiggum explaining how he found the dress
jokes.punchline: Self-aware admission that good police work is unprecedented
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-aware humor acknowledging the character's usual incompetence.

jokes.text: I might get married someday, even if it's only a green card marriage to keep a Chinese dissident from being deported. / We can have the reception at Wong Lee's.
jokes.setup: Lisa reconsidering marriage possibility
jokes.punchline: Highly specific political marriage scenario, Marge's practical response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa moment combining political awareness with romantic cynicism. Marge's practical response is great.

jokes.text: My butt's place is in my pants.
jokes.setup: Bart asking Homer to moon the Google camera
jokes.punchline: Homer's formal, religious restraint about his butt
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer line showing his new religious propriety in the most Homer way possible.

jokes.text: I don't think this is what Martin Presbyluther had in mind when he founded our religion by sticking his Three Suggestions under the Pope's windshield wiper.
jokes.setup: Flanders objecting to the modern church service
jokes.punchline: Mangled Protestant history with modern parking lot imagery
jokes.explanation: Brilliant historical mangling combining Martin Luther, Presbyterian, and modern parking lot annoyance.

jokes.text: I don't understand that on any level, but yes.
jokes.setup: Bart's confusing skateboarding metaphor about Hooper
jokes.punchline: Ned's honest confusion but agreement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned response - polite confusion but still agreeable. The honesty makes it funnier than fake understanding.

jokes.text: You mean he's selling hot tubs? / Yes.
jokes.setup: Description of place full of brimstone and exposed flesh
jokes.punchline: Mundane reality of hot tub sales
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection from hellish description to mundane sales job.

jokes.text: Sir, can you please turn up the bubbles so we can argue in private here?
jokes.setup: Apu and someone having private conversation
jokes.punchline: Using hot tub bubbles as white noise for privacy
jokes.explanation: Creative and absurd solution to privacy problem. Good observational humor about hot tub acoustics.

jokes.text: The only cool thing about my dad was that he hated church. And now he's turned into a fat version of Flanders.
jokes.setup: Bart complaining about Homer's religious transformation
jokes.punchline: Specific comparison to Flanders with physical qualifier
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bart's disappointment in losing his rebellious father figure.

jokes.text: Ugh! One of these bedbugs is still alive. / [Bart steps on it] Much better.
jokes.setup: Bart spreading dead bedbugs for his prank
jokes.punchline: Casual murder of surviving bug
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy moment. Functional but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Ow! He took my contact! / Aah! Now I got two in one eye!
jokes.setup: Frog attack on townspeople
jokes.punchline: Absurd contact lens complications during crisis
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of minor inconvenience during major crisis. Relatable modern problem.

jokes.text: Who here has seen The Blind Side with Sandy Bullock? Remember when Big Mike was overwhelmed with scholarship offers? Well, that's kind of how we are with these frogs.
jokes.setup: Crisis situation requiring leadership
jokes.punchline: Completely inappropriate movie comparison to frog plague
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Hooper's completely inadequate pop culture references for serious situations.

jokes.text: To hell with your references. We're dying here. / I'm more frog than Moe.
jokes.setup: Townsperson rejecting Hooper's movie references
jokes.punchline: Moe's transformation state described matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag setup and Moe's characteristic deadpan acceptance of absurd circumstances.

jokes.text: Um, video games, uh, Twitter. Uh, How to Train Your Dragon. Fight Club. Aah!
jokes.setup: Hooper panicking during crisis
jokes.punchline: Desperate litany of random pop culture references
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Hooper's complete breakdown when his pop culture approach fails.

jokes.text: His words have soothed the amphibious beasts! / His boringness saved us all.
jokes.setup: Lovejoy's traditional psalm calming the frogs
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment crediting his dullness
jokes.explanation: Good ironic reversal making Lovejoy's weakness into his strength.

jokes.text: Four spheres-- two small, two gigantic. / Better zoom in. / Compliments of the deacon.
jokes.setup: Google camera analyzing mysterious shapes
jokes.punchline: Homer's naked body from aerial view with his note
jokes.explanation: Good callback payoff with visual gag. Homer's regression from religious propriety.

tmdb_overview: Homer is recruited to be a deacon by the cutting-edge new pastor. The dry cleaner mistakenly switches Marge's wedding dress for one of Krusty's costumes.
